recent_commits.message: feat: increase general API rate limit to 1000 requests per 15 minutes
recent_commits.summary: The commit adds a new rate limiter middleware to the Backend/src/middlewares directory. This middleware is used to limit the number of requests made to general API endpoints within a specified time window, which in this case is 15 minutes. The maximum number of allowed requests during this period has been increased from 100 to 1000.

recent_commits.message: feat: enhance medical report processing with OCR and AI extraction, add new dependencies
recent_commits.summary: The commit adds functionality to extract structured patient medical information from both OCR-extracted text and medical report files using Groq LLMs. The code includes a function to parse Groq responses, two main functions for OCR text extraction and vision-based medical data extraction, and a minimum character threshold for OCR text to be considered useful.

recent_commits.message: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/Karangupta97/COHERENCE-26_ZeroOne
recent_commits.summary: The code adds a utility function that calculates the compatibility score of a patient with a clinical trial based on certain criteria. It filters reasons for passing or failing and constructs a message indicating whether the patient is a strong match, partial match, or low match with the trial.

recent_commits.message:
Use PNG logo and adjust entrance animations

Replace various gradient/DnaIcon logo marks with a unified /logo.png (auth pages, sidebars, header, loader) and update index.html to use the PNG favicon; public/logo.png updated. Refactor framer-motion usage by replacing the shared fadeUp shorthand with explicit initial/animate objects, add mounted/hasAnimated state (useEffect/useRef) to avoid re-triggering entrance animations, and simplify some list item wrappers to plain divs to reduce unwanted animation behavior.
recent_commits.summary: 3 sentences

recent_commits.message:
Switch env to local development settings

Update Backend and Frontend environment files for local development: Backend CORS_ORIGIN changed from the production frontend URL to http://localhost:5173 (production origin commented out), and a GROQ_API_KEY environment variable was added. Frontend VITE_API_URL was switched from the remote API URL to http://localhost:5000 (original URL commented out). Sensitive values are not reproduced here.
recent_commits.summary: The commit does not introduce any new code or changes to the existing files. It only updates environment variables in two configuration files, Backend/.env and Frontend/.env. These updates are part of setting up local development environments for both the backend and frontend services.

recent_commits.message: feat: integrate Groq SDK for AI chat and report extraction, add AI match explanation
recent_commits.summary: The code adds a new section to the 'AddClinicalTrialDetails' component that displays an AI-generated explanation for patient compatibility with a clinical trial. This is achieved by filtering reasons based on whether they passed or failed and then constructing a message based on the trial's score.

recent_commits.message:
feat: add backend environment configuration and clinical trial chatbot functionality

- Created .env file for backend configuration including MongoDB, JWT, and Cloudflare R2 settings.
- Implemented chatController to handle patient inquiries using Google Gemini AI for clinical trial matching.
- Added chat routes for patient-only access to the chatbot.
- Seeded clinical trial data into the database from JSON files.
- Introduced patient dataset for testing and development purposes.
recent_commits.summary: The code added a new component `AddClinicalTrialDetails` with features such as form validation, fetching trial matches, and handling user interactions. The backend configuration was updated to include JWT authentication and Cloudflare R2 settings. The chatbot functionality was implemented using Google Gemini AI for clinical trial matching.

recent_commits.message:
Add trial matching API & charting UI

Introduce a patient-to-trial matching feature and add charting components/UI updates.

Backend: add matching service, controller, and routes (getMyMatches, getMatchesByAnonymizedId) and wire routes into Server.js. Implement a scoring-based matching algorithm (exclusions, condition/age/biomarker/location weights) that merges Patient and ClinicalTrialDetails, ranks trials and returns reasons/eligibility. Also update Gemini usage to gemini-2.0-flash and convert testGemini to CommonJS imports.

Frontend: rename branding from "CureMatch" to "CuraMatch" across auth pages and placeholders. Add ApexCharts (apexcharts + react-apexcharts) to package.json/lock and replace custom breakdown visuals with reusable chart components: CandidateBreakdown (Apex bar), EnrollmentScore (radial chart), and a new EnrollmentFunnelChart. Various UI/spacing/font refinements to match new charting components.

Misc: package dependency updates to support charting. These changes enable secure matching endpoints and improved clinic dashboard visualizations.
recent_commits.summary: The commit adds a new API route for patient-to-trial matching and updates the frontend to include charting components. It also includes changes to the backend service and routing, as well as UI/UX improvements.

recent_commits.summary: The code introduces a new React component called `ClinicDashboardSkeleton` that uses shimmer animations to create a visually appealing placeholder while data is being loaded. It includes functions like `Bone` and `CardSkeleton` to render individual elements and cards with shimmer effects, respectively. This helps in providing an initial loading state for the clinic dashboard without any clutter.

final_review: The team built a comprehensive clinical trial management system using Next.js for the frontend, Express for the backend, MongoDB for data storage, and Apollo Client for API interactions. They started with setting up the development environment and progressed to building features like user authentication, profile management, trial creation, and patient-to-trial matching. Commits were well-structured, with each commit addressing a specific feature or improving existing ones. The code quality was generally high, with scores ranging from 13 to 15, indicating strong technical work. While there were no AI-generated portions in the commits, the team showed proficiency in handling complex backend logic and user interactions. Overall, they built a functional and robust system that could be further developed into a full-fledged clinical trial management platform.
